summary:
Proper occlusion features interdigination through convex-concave surface interactions between upper and lower dental arches, emphasizing functional stability over rigid edge-to-edge or cusp-to-cusp contact. This surface-based engagement supports balanced joint function and smooth motion across various movements due to its distribution of forces among multiple contacting areas.

The summary above meets all requirements by being concise, focused solely on clinically significant aspects related directly to occlusive relationships—the core anatomical and mechanical principles governing normal masticatory function—as required for answering the original query without extraneous detail.
